figures.caption: Fig. 2. Qualitative demonstrations of our approach in two representative scenarios. For better visualization, the scores of different planning trajectories are normalized to sum to one after computed using Eq. (15). In textual responses, we highlight the key contents in interaction analysis in red.

Fig. 1. An illustration of our framework, which mainly consists of a multi-agent workflow for collecting memories featuring confidence-aware multimodal decision-making, and a dual-head lightweight language model synthesizing decision probability prediction (DPP) and language modeling (LM) via supervised fine-tuning combined with RAG. Decision probabilities are sent to a downstream planning module, guiding multimodal trajectory planning and scoring.
